name: Puente la Pared by the Río Ortiga — round trip
name_original: Puente la Pared por el Río Ortiga —ida y vuelta
region_intro: Jogging around Magacela offers an exploration of the Extremadura region's distinctive stony landscape. The area features diverse paths, including sections with sand terraces and stone, providing varied terrain for runners. Elevated points, such as those near Magacela Castle, offer panoramic views of the surrounding countryside. The region's natural features are complemented by historical landmarks, making for engaging running routes. Best jogging routes around Magacela The most popular jogging route is Doña Blanca Municipal Estate Loop, a 11.3 miles (18.2 km) trail that takes 2 hours 11 minutes to complete. This route explores the Estremeña Dehesa, offering a challenging and extensive running experience. Another top favourite among local runners is Magacela Dolmen and Camino Mozárabe Loop, a moderate 4.4 miles (7.1 km) path. This route combines historical exploration of the Dolmen with sections of the Camino Mozárabe, providing scenic views. Local runners also love the Magacela Castle Loop, a 2.8 miles (4.5 km) trail leading through the historic Magacela Castle area, often completed in about 35 minutes. Jogging around Magacela is defined by its stony landscape, elevated viewpoints, and historical sites like Magacela Castle and the Dolmen. The network of running routes offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 180 runners have used komoot to explore Magacela's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running routes around Magacela?
A: Jogging routes around Magacela feature the typical stony landscape of the Extremadura region. You'll encounter diverse paths, including sections with sand terraces and stone, offering varied terrain for your runs.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in Magacela?
A: Yes, Magacela offers several easy running routes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1283236864/camino-mozarabe-la-haba-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Mozárabe: La Haba Loop&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4.0 km (2.5 miles) trail, perfect for those new to running or looking for a relaxed jog.

Q: Can I find challenging running trails in Magacela?
A: Absolutely. For a more demanding run, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1283247909/dona-blanca-municipal-estate-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Doña Blanca Municipal Estate Loop&lt;/a&gt;. This difficult 18.2 km (11.3 miles) route explores the Estremeña Dehesa, providing an extensive and challenging experience with significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints along the jogging routes in Magacela?
A: Yes, Magacela is known for its panoramic views. Elevated points, such as those near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3035140" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magacela Castle&lt;/a&gt;, offer breathtaking vistas of the surrounding Extremadura landscape. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2458178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de la Cabra Observation Tower&lt;/a&gt; also provides excellent panoramic views and can be a rewarding destination during a run.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see while running in Magacela?
A: Magacela's running routes often incorporate historical sites. You can explore the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3035140" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magacela Castle&lt;/a&gt;, perched atop a steep rocky hill, or visit the well-preserved prehistoric &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2940960" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen de Magacela&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1283283942/magacela-dolmen-and-camino-mozarabe-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magacela Dolmen and Camino Mozárabe Loop&lt;/a&gt; specifically combines historical exploration with scenic running.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in Magacela?
A: Many of the running routes in Magacela are circular, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1283247909/dona-blanca-municipal-estate-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Doña Blanca Municipal Estate Loop&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1283283942/magacela-dolmen-and-camino-mozarabe-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magacela Dolmen and Camino Mozárabe Loop&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1283263133/magacela-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magacela Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: How many running routes are there in Magacela?
A: There are over 35 dedicated running routes around Magacela, offering a wide variety of distances and difficulty levels to suit different preferences.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Magacela?
A: The komoot community frequently praises Magacela for its unique combination of rugged Extremaduran landscapes and rich historical sites. Runners particularly enjoy the panoramic views from elevated points and the diverse paths that offer both physical challenge and cultural exploration.

Q: Are there any running routes that pass by water features?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1283255767/puente-la-pared-by-the-rio-ortiga-round-trip" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente la Pared by the Río Ortiga — round trip&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 12.8 km (8.0 miles) route that allows you to pass over the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5459701" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente la Pared&lt;/a&gt; bridge, offering picturesque views of the Ortiga river.

Q: Are there family-friendly running routes in Magacela?
A: While specific 'family-friendly' designations aren't always explicit, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1283236864/camino-mozarabe-la-haba-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino Mozárabe: La Haba Loop&lt;/a&gt; (4.0 km, easy) or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1283263133/magacela-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Magacela Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt; (4.5 km, moderate) are shorter and less strenuous, making them more suitable for families with children who enjoy outdoor activities.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Magacela?
A: Magacela's climate, typical of Extremadura, suggests that spring and autumn offer the most pleasant temperatures for running. Summers can be very hot, while winters are generally mild but can have cooler mornings. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.
tips: We are at the beautiful La Pared bridge that allows us to cross the Ortiga river and offers us some beautiful views.
Beautiful path that goes along the bank of the Río Ortiga in a path that moves between the bank and the fields, on both sides of the stream.
